paper_content.title: Acquired resistance to a GPRC5D-directed T-cell engager in multiple myeloma is mediated by genetic or epigenetic target inactivation
paper_content.authors: Jennifer Derrien, Sarah Gastineau, Antoine Frigout, Nils Giordano, Mia Cherkaoui, Victor Gaborit, Rémi Boinon, Elise Douillard, Magali Devic, Florence Magrangeas, Philippe Moreau, Stéphane Minvielle, Cyrille Touzeau, Eric Letouzé
paper_content.abstract: Bispecific antibodies targeting GPRC5D demonstrated promising efficacy in multiple myeloma, but acquired resistance usually occurs within a few months. Using a single-nucleus multi-omic strategy in three patients from the MYRACLE cohort (ClinicalTrials.gov registration: NCT03807128), we identified two resistance mechanisms, by bi-allelic genetic inactivation of GPRC5D or by long-range epigenetic silencing of its promoter and enhancer regions. Molecular profiling of target genes may help to guide the choice of immunotherapy and early detection of resistance in multiple myeloma.

plan.key_variants: GPRC5D E27fs, GPRC5D G97-F100del, GPRC5D S125fs, GPRC5D F158fs, GPRC5D W217*, GPRC5D W237*, GPRC5D TSS deletion (9942bp), KRAS G12R, KRAS Q61H, RB1 R787*, RB1 L572fs, CDKN1B N61I, TP53 R267W, SF3B1 R1075K, DUSP2 I240R, DUSP2 H212Y, SETD2 S1965fs, t(4;14), t(11;14)
plan.key_therapies: Talquetamab (GPRC5D-targeting bispecific antibody), Teclistamab (BCMA-targeting), Carfilzomib, Anti-CD38 antibodies, BCMA CAR-T, Immunomodulatory agents, Proteasome inhibitors, Dexamethasone
plan.key_diseases: Multiple myeloma, Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM), IgG myeloma, Light-chain myeloma
plan.focus_sections: Abstract (resistance mechanisms), Results (single-nucleus multi-omic analysis), Discussion (clinical implications), Tables (patient-level variant data), Figures (molecular profiling data). Key focus: sections describing bi-allelic genetic inactivation and epigenetic silencing mechanisms, patient response/resistance timelines, and alternative therapy responses.
plan.extraction_notes: This is a resistance mechanism study from the MYRACLE trial (NCT03807128) examining 3 patients who developed acquired resistance to talquetamab (GPRC5D-directed T-cell engager). PRIMARY EVIDENCE FOCUS: (1) GPRC5D genetic variants (frameshift, nonsense, deletions) conferring RESISTANCE to talquetamab - expect 6-8 items covering different variant types; (2) GPRC5D epigenetic silencing (TSS deletion) conferring RESISTANCE to talquetamab - 1-2 items; (3) Potential PREDICTIVE/PROGNOSTIC evidence for background variants (KRAS, RB1, TP53, SF3B1, translocations) in context of therapy response/resistance. Evidence type will primarily be RESISTANCE (Predictive - Type B). Look for: timing of resistance emergence, variant allele frequencies at resistance, response to alternative therapies post-resistance, correlation between molecular profiles and clinical outcomes. This paper provides mechanistic resistance data that is clinically actionable for immunotherapy selection in myeloma.
